Treasurer to submit the 63 vouchers with complete documentation
totaling ₱8,930,198.19, otherwise, the Audit Team will issue Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance, whichever is more applicable.

the 12 unrecorded checks, with attachments totaling ₱4,815,545.34 to
the Municipal Accountant for recording and submission to the Audit
Team.

2.16 We recommended and Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. request bank statements to LBP and PNB and consequently prepare
the bank reconciliation statements, submit the same to the Office of
the Auditor for review and verification in order to establish the
accuracy of the Cash in Bank balances; and
b. record the adjustments pertaining to Trust Fund-PNB Salintubig
account.

2.17 We further recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to follow-
up the Resolution with the Legislative Office to officially close the non-
moving bank accounts of the Municipality with the PNB and PVB.

3.11 We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a. Diligently prepare, maintain and update the inventory records,
forms and reports, consequently reconcile the same; and
b. Conduct the annual physical count of PPE and prepare the
necessary RPCPPE.

4.5 We recommended and Management agreed that the Accountable
Officer, through the Municipal Treasurer, submit the documentary
requirements for the request from relief of accountability to the Audit
Team for further review.

5.7 We further recommended and Management agreed that the liable
persons refund the total expenses incurred.

6.8 We recommended that Management:
a. stop paying in advance those personnel who are not
officials/employees of the Municipal Government; and
b. require the refund of the cash advances granted to the Dep-Ed
personnel.

8.8 We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a. Submit the lacking requirements as stipulated in the MOA; and
b. Comply with the rules and guidelines stipulated in any agreement in
order to achieve the purpose of the fund.

9.7 We recommended that the BAC strictly comply with the provisions of
R.A. 9184 in the conduct of public bidding to prevent the awarding of
contracts to non-responsive bidder.

10.11 We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the
funding/implementation of its infrastructure projects immediately to
enjoy the benefits therefrom by the beneficiaries.

11.7 We recommended that Management:
a. require the concerned contractors to rectify immediately the
deficiencies;
b. furnish the team of the results of re-validation of the 12 projects
including corrective actions to be undertaken, if any, within five (5)
days after completion of the report; otherwise, a Notice of
Disallowance will be issued; and
c. require the inspectorate team to conduct regular and strict
monitoring and supervision to ensure that contractors implement
projects in accordance with the approved program of works.

12.10 We recommended to the Management the following:
a. Issue Notices to Terminate immediately to the two defaulting
contractors;
b. Follow strictly the procedures of the termination of contracts
prescribed under Item IV of Annex I of the Revised IRR of RA 9184;
and
c. Impose appropriate sanctions to the erring contractors.

13.7 We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Officer (MDRRMO) request only for commodities that
directly respond to the basic needs of the affected individuals.

15.9 We recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to:
a. provide the details and composition of the actual costs of the client-
focused and organization-focused activities reported in GAD
Accomplishment Report in the aggregate amount of ₱19,661,196.00;
and
b. ensure that the GAD Plan, Budget, and Accomplishment Reports are
supported with pertinent documents and other means of verification.

accounts.

Management Officer IV, reinstate a well-designed policy regarding the
attendance of officials and employees at seminars and trainings in order to
foster the continued development of its personnel for the benefit of public
service.

Management Comment

12.7 Concerned official/employees replied to the issued audit observation
memorandum and justified their attendance/participation:

The Honorable Vice-Mayor (VM) explained that in order to enhance the
abilities in legislative and clerical jobs of his staff, sent four of his staff to
undergo training legislative tracking and policy analysis.
Two employees explained that they are allowed to attend LIFT seminar
because they are directly using the system.
One employee attended the seminar at Vigan, Ilocos Sur because his
superior instructed him to attend.
Two personnel from the budget office were not aware that the third quarter
PAGBA Conference includes topics from the second quarter.
The attendance of the staff of the Assessor is deemed necessary since she
is a member of the BAC Technical Working Group (TWG).

Auditor’s Rejoinder

12.8 As to the aforementioned reply, the audit team has reiterated its stance in the
following manner:

The office of the Vice-Mayor staffs which the audit team affirmed its findings
that the training conducted were not related to their duties and
responsibilities. The duties and responsibilities of the four staff are the
following:
a. Two staffs were reassigned to the Office of the Vice-Mayor without
specific duties and responsibilities.
b. On the other hand, one of his staffs is focus more on technical
competencies such as operate reproduction machine and maintain the
same.
c. Similarly, another staff is act as messenger and assist in encoding.
Thus, based on the above, they are not qualified to participate such policy
analysis and legislative tracking training as this is a level of
supervisory/managerial functions.

The invitation to the LIFT system seminar invited only the Municipal
Assessor, Assessor’s Focal Person, Acting Municipal Treasurer and
Statement of Receipts and Expenditures (SRE) Focal Person. However,
instead of the maximum two attendees from each office, three each office
attended the same seminar. The reason for the observation is the
attendance of more than the required which is two.

Verification of entries of the MPDC in his Daily Time Records (DTR)
disclosed that he is in the office on July 3-7 (excluding July 2 and 8 which
falls on Saturdays). Moreover, there was no written consent by the
department head allowing his staff to attend because the communication
specifically noted by the Local Chief Executive that the former will attend
the seminar.

The Budget Officer and her staff's justification is deemed
unacceptable because the interval is only two months, the themes are
identical, and the conference topic is clearly stated in the
invitation/communication.

The audit team could not agree because the staff of the Assessor who is a
member of the BAC TWG by appointment, is not functioning as BAC TWG
before and after issuance of the is not a justifiable reason. A mere member
of the BAC TWG did not entitle her to attend the same because scrutiny of
post-qualification documents on the procurement of goods and supplies
thru public bidding disclosed that there is no single document that she’s
involved in the said post-qualification. Only three persons from the
accounting office conducted the post-qualification.

observations.recommendation:
The Audit Team shall issue a Notice of Disallowance for the invalid claims.

In addition, we recommended the following:
a. The Accounting Office stop obligating and processing payments for
invalid claims, otherwise shall be disallowed in audit; and
b. The Municipal Budget and Accounting Office reconcile the discrepancies
noted and maintain records/ledgers for all obligations incurred and
settled by the municipality.

We recommended that the accounting office record only those obligation that
are valid and authorized.

We further recommended that the accounting office derecognize from the
books all the obligations which are not valid.

The Audit Team recommended the following courses of action:

a. The Municipal Budget Officer maintain records and registry of
appropriation, allotments and obligations to avoid the incurrence of
expenditures in excess of appropriations;

b. The Accounting Office stop processing disbursements not covered by
appropriation; and

c. The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant properly
review the transaction before rendering certification on the availability of
appropriation and allotment, respectively

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. all revenue collectors remit their collections regularly to the Liquidating
Officer;

b. the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer closely monitor the
timely recording, reporting and remittance of collections by the
collectors;

c. Municipal Treasury Office personnel refrain from using their collection
in the encashment of personal checks; and

d. all AOs shall maintain separate cashbooks for General and Trust Fund
accounts.

We recommended that Mr. Basan fully restitute his shortage through remittance to
the Liquidating Officer, otherwise, appropriate legal action will be instituted.

We recommended, as per our letter request dated December 6, 2019 that Mr.
Basan be relieved from his duty as collecting officer and disbursing officer;

We further recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Disbursing
Officer II to immediately settle his shortage in full, otherwise, impose
sanctions or effect the withholding of his salaries;

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the unrecorded
cash advances and refrain from granting cash advances to the CDO if the
previous cash advances were not yet liquidated.

We recommended the following:

a. STOP THE GRANTING CASH ADVANCE TO ELECTED OFFICIALS
for purposes other than their official travelling expenses, otherwise a
Notice of Disallowance will be issued; and

b. Grant cash advances only to designated disbursing officers who are
properly bonded and only for purposes that are allowed by law.

We recommended that management STOP granting cash advances to officers
and employees with outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that Management ensure that all disbursements are
completely supported to establish their validity and legality.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat consolidate Purchase Requests
of similar items approved at or about the same period to avoid splitting of
contracts, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all Programs,
Activities and Projects (PAPs) identified in the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Investment Plan (MDRRMIP) are fully
implemented in the year they are appropriated in order to reduce the risk of
disaster and mitigate the impact of calamities, man-made or caused by
natural phenomenon.

observations.recommendation:
1.12 We recommended that management:
a) conduct physical inventory of all properties of the LGU annually and
submit annual physical inventory report of Property Plant and
Equipment ;
b) ensure that all properties of the municipality issued to officers and employees
shall be covered by Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR), which shall be
renewed every January of the third year after issue;
c) require the Accounting Department to maintain a separate updated
subsidiary ledgers of property plant and equipment to support the
general ledger accounts to ensure completeness of the recorded amounts in
the books of accounts; and
d) require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain property cards and
reconcile regularly with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that an accurate
record of the agency’s Furniture, Fixtures
& Equipment be established with the
following procedures:
a. The municipal accountant shall
maintain subsidiary ledgers, update the
same and reconcile the property balances
with the general ledger.
b. The municipal treasurer shall
maintain property cards for each class of
fixed asset and reconcile their balances
with the equipment ledger cards
maintained by the municipal accountant.
c. The Acknowledgment Receipts for
Equipment (ARE) shall be renewed every
three years or every time there is a
change of accountability to facilitate
pinpointing responsibility.
d. Actual physical count of Furniture,
Fixtures and Equipment shall be
conducted to determine their existence,
conditions and whereabouts and the
municipal treasurer shall be required to
prepare the report on the Physical Count
of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) and submit a copy of the
report to the auditor for verification.
